text:
HEMATOPATHOLOGY вЂ” BONE MARROW BIOPSY AND ASPIRATE
POST-ALLOGENEIC STEM CELL TRANSPLANT ENGRAFTMENT ASSESSMENT

в”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђ
PATIENT: Marcus T. Delgado
MRN: [REDACTED]
DOB: [REDACTED]  AGE: 48 years
SEX: Male
ORDERING PROVIDER: BMT Attending, Hematology/Oncology
CLINICAL SERVICE: Blood and Marrow Transplant (BMT)
SPECIMEN ACCESSION NUMBER: [REDACTED]
в”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђ

CLINICAL INDICATION:
Acute myeloid leukemia (AML), de novo, with FLT3-ITD (high allelic ratio, AR 0.87) and DNMT3A R882H co-mutation; ELN 2022 adverse-risk. Status post 7+3 induction chemotherapy (cytarabine/daunorubicin) plus midostaurin, with documented complete morphologic remission and MRD negativity (multiparameter flow cytometry, 10^-4 sensitivity) following induction. Subsequently underwent myeloablative allogeneic hematopoietic stem cell transplantation (allo-HSCT) with a 10/10 HLA-matched unrelated donor (MUD), conditioned with fludarabine/busulfan (FluBu4). GVHD prophylaxis with tacrolimus and short-course methotrexate. G-CSF-mobilized peripheral blood stem cell product infused on day 0, CD34+ cell dose 5.8 x 10^6/kg. Neutrophil engraftment documented on day +14 (ANC >500/uL x 3 consecutive days). This biopsy is performed at day +30 for formal engraftment confirmation, donor chimerism assessment, and disease re-evaluation.

в”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђ
SPECIMEN(S) SUBMITTED:
  A: Right posterior iliac crest core biopsy
  B: Left posterior iliac crest core biopsy
  C: Bilateral iliac crest aspirate smears (Wright-Giemsa stained, 10 slides prepared)
  D: Aspirate clot section
  E: Touch imprint preparations from core biopsies
в”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђ

DATE OF PROCEDURE: [DATE REDACTED вЂ” TO BE ADDED PROGRAMMATICALLY]
DATE OF REPORT: [DATE REDACTED вЂ” TO BE ADDED PROGRAMMATICALLY]
PATHOLOGIST: Hematopathology Attending, Department of Pathology and Laboratory Medicine

в”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђ
GROSS DESCRIPTION:

Specimen A (Right iliac crest core): Received in formalin, labeled with patient name and specimen designation. The specimen consists of a tan-white bone marrow core biopsy measuring 1.8 cm in length x 0.2 cm in diameter. The core is intact, firm, and demonstrates adequate trabecular bone. Submitted entirely in one cassette for decalcification and paraffin embedding.

Specimen B (Left iliac crest core): Received in formalin, labeled with patient name and specimen designation. The specimen consists of a tan-white bone marrow core biopsy measuring 1.6 cm in length x 0.2 cm in diameter. The core is intact, firm. Submitted entirely in one cassette for decalcification and paraffin embedding.

Specimen C (Aspirate smears): Ten Wright-Giemsa stained aspirate smears received, labeled with patient name. Smears are of adequate cellularity with spicules present on the majority of slides. Differential count performed on 500 cells from a representative smear.

Specimen D (Aspirate clot section): A tan-pink aspirate clot measuring 0.5 x 0.4 x 0.3 cm is submitted in toto in one cassette.

Specimen E (Touch imprint preparations): Four touch imprint slides prepared at bedside and stained with Wright-Giemsa; submitted for review.

в”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђ
MICROSCOPIC DESCRIPTION:

CORE BIOPSY (Specimens A and B):
Sections from both cores are adequate for interpretation and demonstrate comparable findings; description below is composite.

Cellularity: The marrow is hypercellular to normocellular, with an estimated overall cellularity of 60вЂ“70% (fat-to-cell ratio approximately 35:65). This represents a marked increase in cellularity compared to the prior day +28-30 post-induction biopsy (which showed 5вЂ“10% cellularity consistent with aplasia), and is expected at day +30 post-transplant with documented engraftment.

Hematopoiesis: Trilineage hematopoiesis is present with ongoing reconstitution. All three lineages вЂ” erythroid, myeloid, and megakaryocytic вЂ” are identified and show evidence of progressive maturation.

  ERYTHROPOIESIS: Erythroid islands (normoblastic clusters) are present throughout the biopsy sections. Predominantly intermediate and late normoblasts are seen, with orderly maturation sequence. No megaloblastic change. No dyserythropoiesis (no binucleation, nuclear budding, or cytoplasmic bridging identified). Early erythroid precursors (proerythroblasts) are also present in small numbers. The erythroid compartment appears appropriately reconstituting.

  MYELOPOIESIS: Myeloid series demonstrates regeneration with a left-shifted but maturing granulocytic population. Myeloblasts, promyelocytes, myelocytes, metamyelocytes, and band/segmented neutrophils are all represented. The myeloid-to-erythroid (M:E) ratio is estimated at approximately 3:1, within expected range for early post-engraftment marrow. No aberrant or dysplastic myeloid forms. No Pelger-Huet-like changes. No hypersegmented neutrophils (consistent with no folate/B12 deficiency in this methotrexate-conditioned patient recovering at this time point). Eosinophils and basophils present in small numbers, morphologically unremarkable.

  MEGAKARYOPOIESIS: Megakaryocytes are present, adequate in number (estimated 3вЂ“5 per 10x field in representative areas), and demonstrate a range of maturity from small hypolobated forms (expected in early engraftment) to larger, multilobed mature forms. No micromegakaryocytes. No nuclear hyperchromasia or dysplastic megakaryocytic clustering abnormalities identified.

Blasts: Morphologic blast count is estimated at less than 5% by differential count on aspirate smears and less than 1% by visual estimate on core biopsy sections. No Auer rods identified. No aberrant blast clusters.

Stroma: The marrow stroma is intact. Sinusoids are patent. No evidence of hemorrhage, necrosis, or edema suggestive of sinusoidal obstruction syndrome (SOS/VOD). Fat cells partially reconstituting.

Osseous Trabeculae: Bony trabeculae are intact, with normal lamellar architecture. No lytic or blastic changes. No evidence of osteonecrosis.

ASPIRATE SMEARS (Specimen C) вЂ” 500-cell differential:
  Myeloblasts: 2%
  Promyelocytes: 4%
  Myelocytes/metamyelocytes: 18%
  Band/segmented neutrophils: 32%
  Eosinophils: 3%
  Basophils: <1%
  Monocytes: 6%
  Lymphocytes: 14%
  Plasma cells: 1%
  Erythroid precursors: 20%
  M:E ratio: approximately 4:1
  Comment: No morphologically abnormal blasts identified. Occasional lymphocytes with large granular morphology noted (donor-origin NK/T cells, expected post-transplant). No plasma cell clusters. No hemophagocytosis.

TOUCH IMPRINTS: Consistent with findings above. Adequate hematopoietic elements present. No morphologically identifiable leukemic blasts.

в”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђ
SPECIAL STAINS:

Reticulin (silver impregnation): Grade 0вЂ“1 fibrosis (MF-0 to MF-1 by European Consensus grading). Thin reticulin fibers present without significant thickening or crossing. No collagen fibrosis. This represents normal or near-normal reticulin architecture, improved compared to potential early post-chemotherapy changes. No evidence of marrow fibrosis.

Giemsa stain (aspirate): Adequate trilineage elements as described above.

PAS stain (core section): Erythroid precursors show normal PAS reactivity. No ring sideroblasts or abnormal PAS positivity in blast population to suggest erythroleukemia.

Iron stain (Prussian blue, aspirate): Storage iron present (grade 2+, within acceptable limits). Sideroblasts present (estimated 20-25% ring sideroblast precursors, sideroblasts without ring morphology). Note: Post-transplant patients frequently show transient sideroblastic changes and relative iron excess; this finding alone is not diagnostic of sideroblastic anemia in this clinical setting. No ring sideroblasts (no sideroblasts with 5 or more iron granules encircling >1/3 of nucleus) identified.

в”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђ
IMMUNOHISTOCHEMISTRY (performed on formalin-fixed paraffin-embedded core biopsy sections):

CD34 (QBEnd-10): Scattered CD34-positive cells identified, comprising an estimated 1вЂ“2% of marrow cellularity. These cells are dispersed singly and in very small clusters of 2вЂ“3 cells, consistent with normal hematopoietic progenitor distribution. No abnormal CD34-positive blast aggregates or clusters are identified. No paratrabecular or intratrabecular blast collections.

CD117 (c-Kit): CD117 positivity seen in scattered progenitor cells and mast cells, co-localizing with CD34+ progenitors and normal mast cell elements. No aberrant CD117 over-expression or blast accumulation identified.

MPO (myeloperoxidase): Diffuse staining across maturing myeloid elements, consistent with progressive granulocytic reconstitution. No aberrant MPO expression in blast-appearing cells.

CD3 (SP7): T-lymphocytes present, scattered throughout the interstitium. Estimated 10вЂ“15% of lymphoid cells are CD3-positive. No large T-cell aggregates or aberrant T-cell populations. Note: Donor-derived T cells are expected at this post-transplant time point.

CD20 (L26): Small B-lymphocytes present, scattered singly. No CD20+ lymphoid aggregates. B-cell reconstitution is expected to be limited at day +30 post-transplant (normal finding).

CD56 (123C3): Scattered NK cells present in small numbers; no aberrant NK-cell clusters.

CD138 (plasma cell marker): Rare plasma cells, less than 1% of marrow cellularity, scattered distribution. No plasma cell clusters.

TdT: Negative. No immature lymphoid blasts identified.

CD64 / CD14: Negative on blast-appearing cells (no monocytic differentiation of residual blasts; background monocytes stain as expected).

в”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђ
ANCILLARY STUDIES:

1. CHIMERISM ANALYSIS (STR-based short tandem repeat polymorphism, whole blood and sorted cell fractions) вЂ” RESULTS AVAILABLE:
   - Whole blood chimerism: 97% DONOR (3% recipient)
   - CD3+ T-cell fraction chimerism: 91% DONOR (9% recipient)
   - CD33+ myeloid cell fraction chimerism: 99% DONOR (<1% recipient)
   INTERPRETATION: Near-complete donor chimerism established in myeloid compartment. Residual mixed chimerism in T-cell fraction is expected at day +30 post-transplant with tacrolimus-based GVHD prophylaxis and is being monitored closely. No immediate intervention required. Serial chimerism monitoring recommended.

2. MOLECULAR STUDIES вЂ” FLT3-ITD PCR (fragment analysis, marrow aspirate; sensitivity ~10^-3 to 10^-4):
   RESULT: NEGATIVE for FLT3-ITD mutation.
   INTERPRETATION: No detectable FLT3-ITD sequence at this sensitivity level. This is consistent with donor-origin hematopoiesis and ongoing complete molecular remission.

3. MULTIPARAMETER FLOW CYTOMETRY (MRD assessment, marrow aspirate; 10-color panel; sensitivity 10^-4) вЂ” RESULTS PENDING AT TIME OF FINAL REPORT SIGN-OUT. Results to be reported as addendum when available.

4. DNMT3A R882H DIGITAL DROPLET PCR (ddPCR) вЂ” RESULTS PENDING AT TIME OF FINAL REPORT SIGN-OUT. Results to be reported as addendum when available.

5. CYTOGENETICS / CONVENTIONAL KARYOTYPE (G-banding, aspirate) вЂ” RESULTS PENDING AT TIME OF FINAL REPORT SIGN-OUT. Results to be reported as addendum when available. Prior karyotype at diagnosis and post-induction remission assessment: 46,XY [20/20 metaphases], normal male karyotype.

в”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђ
DIAGNOSIS:

AвЂ“E.  BONE MARROW BIOPSY (BILATERAL POSTERIOR ILIAC CREST) AND ASPIRATE, DAY +30 POST-ALLOGENEIC HEMATOPOIETIC STEM CELL TRANSPLANT:

  1. POST-TRANSPLANT BONE MARROW WITH TRILINEAGE ENGRAFTMENT AND PROGRESSIVE HEMATOPOIETIC RECONSTITUTION.
     - Marrow cellularity 60вЂ“70%; all three lineages present and maturing.
     - Morphologic blast count LESS THAN 5% (estimated <1% on core, 2% on aspirate differential).
     - No morphologic evidence of residual or recurrent acute myeloid leukemia.
     - No Auer rods identified.

  2. NEAR-COMPLETE DONOR CHIMERISM BY STR ANALYSIS:
     - Whole blood: 97% donor.
     - CD33+ myeloid fraction: 99% donor.
     - CD3+ T-cell fraction: 91% donor (mixed, expected post-transplant).

  3. FLT3-ITD PCR: NEGATIVE.

  4. RETICULIN FIBROSIS: Grade 0вЂ“1 (MF-0/MF-1); no significant fibrosis.

  5. MULTIPARAMETER FLOW CYTOMETRY MRD вЂ” PENDING (addendum to follow).

  6. DNMT3A R882H ddPCR вЂ” PENDING (addendum to follow).

  7. CONVENTIONAL CYTOGENETICS вЂ” PENDING (addendum to follow).

в”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђ
COMMENT:
This bone marrow at day +30 post-allogeneic transplant demonstrates well-established trilineage engraftment with near-complete donor chimerism in the myeloid compartment. There is no morphologic evidence of residual or recurrent AML. Blast count is within normal limits on both aspirate differential and core biopsy immunohistochemistry (CD34+ cells <2%, normal distribution). FLT3-ITD PCR is negative, further supporting molecular remission. The residual 9% recipient contribution in the T-cell chimerism fraction at this early time point is consistent with expected kinetics of T-cell reconstitution under tacrolimus-based GVHD prophylaxis; this is not indicative of leukemic relapse. Pending MRD by flow cytometry and DNMT3A R882H ddPCR will provide additional sensitivity for minimal residual disease detection and will be reported as an addendum. Conventional cytogenetics are pending. Clinical correlation with current CBC and peripheral blood counts is recommended.

Clinical history reviewed and incorporated into interpretation.

Electronically signed by: Hematopathology Attending
Department of Pathology and Laboratory Medicine
[Signature and institutional stamp]

в”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђ
ADDENDUM (to be appended when pending studies result):
  [ ] Multiparameter flow cytometry MRD result
  [ ] DNMT3A R882H ddPCR result
  [ ] Conventional karyotype result
